channel.title: Pies on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Pies on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: “dirt” Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 pkg (4-serving size) Jello Chocolate
  • 1 cup cold milk
  • instant pudding
  • 1 1-L Cool Whip, thawed
  • 20 chocolate sandwich cookies
  • 1-1/2 cups miniature marshmallows
  • chopped
  • 1 graham crumb crust
  • Gummy worms for decoration

Instructions

Prepare pudding according to package directions reducing milk to 1 cup. Fold in whipped topping. Stir in 2/3 of the cookies and all the marshmallows. Spoon into crust. Sprinkle top with remaining cookies. Decorate with gummy worms. Freeze until firm – about 4 hours. Remove from freezer 10 minutes before serving for easy slicing. Makes 6 to 8 servings. For best results, use only Jello instant pudding and Cool Whip topping.

item.title: Apple Pie – (purity Cookbook)
item.description:
Instructions

Preheat over to 375 degrees.

For each pie, I combine slightly more than ½ cup of sugar (more if apples are tart) with ½ tsp cinnamon and a dash of salt.

Prepare pastry and line a 9” pie plate with the rolled pastry..Roll out the top pastry.

Peel and core apples. (I usually used 6 medium-sized apples). I half fill the pastry-lined pie plate with thinly sliced apples. Sprinkle on half of the sugar mixture. Finish filling the pie plate with thinly sliced apples, heaping them in the center. Sprinkle the rest of the sugar mixture on top. Dot with 1 Tablespoon of butter.

item.title: Beverly’s Spinach Quiche
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 box chopped spinach or broccoli
  • l/2 chopped onion
  • l/2 cup bell pepper
  • l cup cheese
  • ¾ cup Bisquick
  • 3 eggs
  • l/2 tsp salt
  • l/4 tsp pepper

Instructions

Spray Pam in pan. Put first 4 ingredients (spinach, onion, bell pepper, cheese) in bottom of pie plate. Mix rest with mixer to pour over other ingredients. Bake at 400 degrees for 35 minutes.

item.title: Butter Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup soft butter
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2 tbsp milk
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • raisins or walnuts (optional)

Instructions

Cream butter and brown sugar. Add milk, vanilla and egg, and beat well, preferably with an electric beater. Half fill pastry-lined tart tins and bake in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes.

item.title: Butterscotch Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 tbsp butter
  • 1-1/2 cups of milk
  • 1-1/2 cups brown sugar
  • 6 tbsp flour
  • ¾ cup boiling water
  • pinch of salt
  • 4 egg yolks

Instructions

Melt butter, add sugar and allow to brown. Gradually add boiling water. In medium size bowl, beat egg yolks; add a small amount of the measured milk and the flour. Beat until smooth. Then add remainder of milk and salt. Pour this slowly into sugar mixture and cook until thick, stirring constantly.

item.title: Butterscotch Pie (carmen Zimmerman)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 9”baked pie shell
  • 2-1/2 cups milk
  • 1 cup dark brown sugar
  • 3 egg yolks, slightly beaten
  • 3-1/2 tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 3 tbsp butter

Instructions

Combine sugar, cornstarch and salt in top of a double boiler. Stir in cold milk. Cook over boiling water until thickened – stirring constantly. Cover and cook 15 minutes longer.

Stir a little of the hot mixture into slightly beaten egg yolks. Add to remaining mixture in double boiler and cook for 2 minutes over hot, not boiling, water, stirring constantly. Add the butter and vanilla and let cool. Pour into the baked pie shell.

item.title: Chicken Pie – (merit)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 tin cream of chicken soup
  • 1 cup cubed cooked chicken
  • ½ cup milk or water
  • Mashed potatoes

Instructions

Place ingredients in a pan. Then cover with mashed potatoes. Heat in oven at 325 degrees for 30 minutes.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Never Fail Hot Water Partry
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups flour
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 lb Tenderflake lard
  • 1 cup boiling water

Instructions

Combine dry ingredients. Cut lard into it until grainy. Add water. Mix thoroughly. Wrap and set 24 hours. Take out 6 hours before use. Will last 3 months in fridge, 6 months in freezer.

Makes 4 2-crust pies.

NOTE: originated in England approx. 150 years ago. If you want to make it fussy for fruit pies, roll top crust in sugar.

item.title: Frontier Kitchen Sugar Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 eggs
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 Tbsp cream or milk
  • 3 Tbsp melted butter
  • 1 Tbsp cornstarch
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Mix all together. Then place in uncooked tart shells and bake at 400 F. until pie is golden brown.

item.title: Glazed Berry Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 baked 9- or 10-inch pie shell
  • 5 cups blackberries, strawberries, raspberries
  • 3 tbsp cornstarch
  • or blueberries
  • 1 cup sugar
  • ¾ cup water
  • 1 tsp lemon juice
  • 1 cup whipping cream

Instructions

Crush and sieve 1 cup of the berries to remove seeds. Combine cornstarch, sugar, sieved berries, and water. Cook over medium-low heat until thickened and clear, about 10 minutes. Cool slightly, then add lemon juice and remaining berries. Mix lightly and pile into baked pie shell. Cool and chill about 2 hours. At serving time, whip the cream and pipe or spoon around the rim.

item.title: Impossible Bacon Breakfast Pie (quiche)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb bacon
  • l can green chili (chopped)
  • l cup grated cheese (Jack)
  • l-l/2 cups milk
  • l cup Bisquick
  • 6 oz sour cream
  • 3 eggs
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions

Brown, drain and crumble bacon. Put in bottom of ll”x 7”greased dish. Top with onion and cheese. Pour rest in blender for l minute. Pour into pan. Bake at 350 degrees until tests done with a knife. (35 – 45 minutes.)

item.title: Impossible Breakfast Quiche
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ½ cup Bisquick
  • Dash pepper
  • ½ cup butter – melted
  • ½ cup sausage cooked and crumbled
  • 1-1/2 cups milk

Instructions

Blend all but cheese and meat for 15 seconds in blender. Sprinkle with cheese and meat on top. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes. Cool for 5 minutes.

item.title: Impossible Chicken ‘n Broccoli Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 10-oz pkg frozen chopped broccoli
  • 1-1/3 cup shredded Cheddar Cheese 8 oz.
  • 1-1/2 cup cooked chopped chicken
  • 2/3 cup chopped onion
  • 1-1/3 cup milk
  • 3 eggs
  • ¾ cup Bisquick
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate 10 x 1-1/2”. Rinse broccoli in cold water to thaw; drain thoroughly. Mix broccoli, cheese, the chicken and onion in pie plate. Beat milk, eggs, baking mix, salt and pepper 15 seconds in blender on high speed, 1 minute with wire whisk or hand beater or until smooth. Pour into pie plate. Bake 30 to 35 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes.

item.title: Impossible Chicken Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cut-up chicken
  • ½ - 1 can mushrooms, drained
  • ½ cup diced green onions
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 cup shredded Swiss or brick cheese
  • 1-1/2 cup milk
  • ¾ cup Bisquick

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate and sprinkle with flour. Into pie plate, place the chicken, mushrooms, onions, salt and cheese. Beat remaining ingredients until smooth. Pour over mixture in pie plate. Bake until knife is clean when inserted into center of pie – 30 to 35 minutes. If you wish you can sprinkle top of pie with Parmesan cheese or corn flake crumbs.

item.title: Impossible Coconut Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups milk
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • ½ cup biscuit mix
  • 4 eggs
  • ¼ cup butter or margarine
  • 1-1/2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup flaked coconut

Instructions

Combine milk, sugar, biscuit mix, eggs, butter and vanilla in electric blender container. Cover and blend on low speed for 3 minutes. Pour into greased pie plate. Let stand about 5 minutes, then sprinkle with coconut. Bake at 350 degrees for 40 minutes. Serve warm or cold.

item.title: Impossible French Apple Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 6 cups sliced pared tart apples
  • ¾ cup milk
  • 1-1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
  • ½ cup Bisquick
  • ¼ tsp ground nutmeg
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 2 tbsp soft margarine or butter
  • Streusel (Below)

Instructions

Heat oven to 325 degrees. Grease pie plate 10xl-l/2”. Mix apples and spices, turn into plate. Beat remaining ingredients except Streusel until smooth, 15 seconds in blender on high or l minute with hand beater. Pour into plate. Sprinkle with Streusel. Bake until knife inserted in center comes out clean, 55 to 60 minutes. STREUSEL;Mix 1 cup Bisquick baking mix, ½ cup chopped nuts, 1/3 cup packed brown sugar and 3 tbsp firm margarine or butter until crumbly.

item.title: Impossible Ham ‘n Cheese Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups cut-up fully cooked smoked ham
  • 4 eggs
  • 1 cup shredded natural Swiss cheese
  • 2 cups milk
  • 1/3 cup chopped green onion or onion
  • 1 cup Bisquick
  • ¼ tsp salt, if desired
  • 1/8 tsp pepper

Instructions

Heat oven to 400 degrees. Grease pie plate, 10 x 1-1/2”. Sprinkle ham, cheese and onion in plate. Beat remaining ingredients until smooth, 15 seconds in blender on high or 1 minute with hand beater. Pour into plate. Bake until golden brown and knife inserted in center comes out clean, 35 to 40 minutes. Cool 5 minutes. 6 servings.

item.title: Impossible Lasagne Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup creamed cottage cheese
  • 1 lb ground beef
  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp dried oregano
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 can (6 oz) tomato paste
  • 2 eggs
  • ½ cup tea biscuit mix

Instructions

Spread cottage cheese in greased 9” pie plate. Cook ground beef, drain. Stir in ½ cup mozzarella cheese, salt, oregano and tomato paste. Spoon over cottage cheese. Stir milk, eggs and biscuit mix with fork until blended. Pour into pie plate. Bake 30-35 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake 1 – 2 minutes more until cheese is melted. What could be simpler? And follow this up with a dessert that’s just as easy. IMPOSSIBLE PUMPKIN PIE1

item.title: Impossible Pumpkin Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 can (16 oz) pumpkin
  • ½ cup tea biscuit mix
  • 2-1/2 tsp pumpkin pie spice
  • 2 tbsp margarine
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 can evaporated milk
  • 3 eggs

Instructions

Put everything in a blender. Mix well. Grease a 9”pie plate. Fill. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 45 – 50 minutes.

item.title: M.n.r. Sugar Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup brown sugar
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • ½ cup flour
  • 1 pie shell, uncooked
  • 1-1/4 cup cream

Instructions

Mix all ingredients. Pour in pie crust. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 350 degrees and bake another 25 – 30 minutes until filling is set. Cool before serving

Tastes great with scoop of ice cream.

item.title: Maple Butter Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 recipe pastry
  • 1/3 cup butter or margarine
  • ½ cup maple syrup
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup raisins or walnuts
  • 2 eggs, slightly beaten

Instructions

Prepare pastry. Line medium tart tins. Combine remaining ingredients. Mix well. Fill tart tins 2/3 full. Bake at 350 degrees for about 25-30 minutes. Makes about 16 medium tarts.

item.title: Never-fail Pie Crust
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 5 cups flour
  • 2 tbsp white sugar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 2 tbsp vinegar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 lb lard

Instructions

Sift dry ingredients. Add lard and cut into the flour until lard is in very small pieces. In an 8 oz cup, beat egg. Add vinegar and fill cup with cold water. Combine wet and dry mixtures. Use plenty of flour when rolling pie crust. Store unused pie crust in covered container in the refrigerator or separate into smaller batches and freeze.

item.title: Prize Butter Tarts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup butter
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg, beaten
  • 2 tbsp milk

Instructions

Melt butter, mix all ingredients and put in tart shells. Bake at 350 degrees until pastry is browned – approx. 30 minutes.

item.title: Pumpkin Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • l tbsp pumpkin pie spice
  • l tbsp all-purpose flour
  • l/2 tsp salt
  • 2 eggs
  • l-l/2 cups pumpkin
  • l-l/2 cups milk (can be part cream)

Instructions

Mix sugar, spice, flour and salt. Beat eggs until light and fluffy. Stir in pumpkin. Add dry ingredients, mixing well. Gradually stir in milk. Blend thoroughly. Bake in pie pastry in a 375 degree oven for 45 – 55 minutes.

item.title: Quiche Crust (rice)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • l-l/2 cups cooked brown rice
  • 2 tbsp butter, softened
  • 2 tbsp chopped onion
  • l/4 tsp basil
  • l egg, slightly beaten

Instructions

Combine ingredients. Press into bottom and sides of greased 9” pie plate. Use any quiche filling. Tuna tastes especially good in this crust.

item.title: Rhubarb Cream Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups sugar
  • 4 tbsp flour
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 2 beaten eggs
  • 3 cups cut-up rhubarb
  • 1 unbaked pie shell

Instructions

Blend sugar, flour, nutmeg and butter. Add beaten eggs. Beat until smooth. Pour over rhubarb in a 9-inch pastry lined pan. Top with pastry if desired. Bake 10 minutes at 450 degrees, then reduce heat to 350 degrees and bake for 30 minutes.

NOTE: This is the original recipe which I copied. However, I bake this pie for about one hour at 375 degrees.

item.title: Richard Belanger’s Sugar Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 250-gr whipping cream
  • ¼ - ½ cup of Carnation milk
  • 1 full cup brown sugar
  • ¼ cup flour
  • Vanilla and nutmeg

Instructions

Mix all together at minimum speed and then at full speed until well blended.

Pour into uncooked pie shell and bake at 350 until “you decide it’s cooked”.

P.S. I have not made this yet but I understand it’s a prize winner. When I do make this pie, I will time it and enter it on this sheet.

item.title: Sour Cream Apple Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup dairy sour cream
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 3 tbsp all-purpose flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 egg
  • 4 cups peeled, cored & diced tart apples
  • 1 9”unbaked pastry shell
  • 1/3 cup packed brown sugar
  • ½ cup all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup butter

Instructions

Beat together cream, sugar, flour, salt, vanilla and egg. Add apples. Pour into unbaked pastry shell. Bake in 375 degree oven for 25 minutes.

item.title: Sour Cream Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup thick sour cream
  • ½ cup sugar
  • 1 cup raisin
  • 1-1/2 tbsp flour
  • 2 eggs, separated
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • Few grains nutmeg
  • Few grains salt

Instructions

Mix together raisins, sugar, flour, salt and spices. Add to sour cream. Mix and add well beaten egg yolks. Whip egg whites until stiff; fold into mixture. Pour mixture into an unbaked pie shell. Bake in a moderate oven – 350 degrees – until filling is firm enough not to stick to a knife inserted into center of pie- about 50 minutes.

item.title: Southern Pecan Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • 1/3 cup melted butter or margarine
  • 2/3 cups sugar
  • 1 cup pecan halves
  • 1 cup dark corn syrup
  • 9-inch unbaked pastry shell

Instructions

Beat eggs thoroughly with sugar, dash of salt, corn syrup and melted butter. Add pecans. Pour into unbaked pastry shell. Bake in moderate oven at 350 degrees for 50 minutes or until knife inserted halfway between outside and center of filling comes out clean. Cool.

item.title: Sparkling Strawberry Pie - Cw
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 pkg (3 oz ea.) raspsberry or strawberry-
  • 2 cups boiling water
  • flavoured Jello
  • 3-4 cups frozen whole strawberries
  • Whipped topping
  • 1 prepared pie crust

Instructions

In a mixing bow, dissolve gelatin in water. Add frozen berries and allow to set a few minutes to thicken. When partially set, spoon into pie shell. Refrigerate. Garnish with whipped topping.

item.title: Sugar Pie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup evaporated milk or cream
  • 2 eggs
  • 4 Tbsp flour
  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Beat eggs with milk. Add remaining ingredients and blend well. Pour into an unbaked 9-inch pie shell. Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 40 minutes

item.title: Tarte Au Sucre (pauline Dupuis)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups brown sugar
  • 2 eggs, beaten
  • 2 tbsp margarine
  • 3 tbsp flour
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1-1/2 cups milk

Instructions

Mix together all ingredients and pour into a large uncooked pie shell or two small ones. Bake at 375 degrees for approx. 50 minutes.

item.title: The Ultimate Tourtiere
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 lb ground pork (lean or medium)
  • ¾ cup water
  • ¾ cup finely chopped onion
  • ¼ cup finely chopped celery
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • ¾ tsp salt
  • ½ tsp dried savory
  • ¼ tsp each thyme, pepper and ground cloves
  • 1 bay leaf
  • ½ cup mashed potato
  • Pastry for double crust 9”pie

Instructions

In large heavy saucepan, combine pork, water, onions, celery and garlic. Cook over medium heat until bubbling, stirring to break up meat. Add salt, savory, thyme, pepper, cloves and bay leaf. Reduce heat, cover and simmer, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes. Remove bay leaf. Stir in mashed potato, mixing well. Taste and add more salt and pepper if needed. Let cool, stirring occasionally (mixture will thicken as it cools). Line pie plate with pastry. Fill with meat mixture. Cover with remaining pastry, seal and crimp edges, cut a few steam vents. Bake in a 425 degree oven for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 375 degrees and bake for 20 to 25 minutes longer or until golden brown.

item.title: Tourtiere
item.description:
Instructions

If you don’t have your own favorite meat pie, here is a recipe you might like to consider

Pastry for a 2-crust 9”pie.

Filling: 1 Tbsp shortening 2 – 3 Tbsp boiling water 1 small onion, chopped fine Salt and pepper ½ lb lean veal, minced Celery leaves or parsley snipped ½ lb minced pork Enough crumbs to thicken the meat

Method: Saute the onion in melted shortening until limp. Add pork, veal and water. Simmer 35 minutes or just until meat becomes very tender. Add salt, pepper, celery or parsley and crumbs. Bake at 375 degrees for about 45 – 50 minutes or until crust is nicely browned.
